intitule: Household Chores Vocabulary and Phrasal Verbs

enonce.texte: Read the sentences below and complete them with the correct phrasal verb or household chore vocabulary.
images: An illustration showing a person cleaning a room, washing dishes, and sweeping the floor.

questions.texte: Match the phrasal verbs with their meanings.
questions.reponse: The correct matching will be provided below.

sousQuestions.reponse: to take care of someone or something
to find information (e.g., in a dictionary or online)
to investigate or examine a situation
to think someone is less important

questions.texte: Complete the following sentences with the correct phrasal verb from the list: look after, look for, look up, look into, look down
questions.reponse: The correct answers are provided below.

sousQuestions.texte: I need to ______ my little brother while my parents are at work.
Can you please ______ the meaning of this word in the dictionary?
We must ______ the problem of garbage disposal in our neighborhood.
She is ______ her keys. She can't find them.
We should not ______ on people who are less fortunate.

enonce.texte:
Listen to the instructions (or read them below) and answer the questions that follow.

How to Clean a Bathroom

Cleaning the bathroom is important for health and hygiene. First, gather all your supplies: a broom, a mop, cleaning solution, gloves, a scrub brush, and some cloths. Put on your gloves to protect your hands. Next, remove all items from the sink, bathtub, and floor. Sweep the floor to remove dust and dirt. Then, apply the cleaning solution to the sink and bathtub. Use the scrub brush to remove any stains. Rinse with water and wipe dry with a clean cloth. Finally, mop the floor to make it shine. Don't forget to put everything back in its place.
images: An illustration showing the step-by-step process of cleaning a bathroom with tools and supplies.

questions.texte: According to the text, why is cleaning the bathroom important?
questions.reponse: Cleaning the bathroom is important for health and hygiene.

questions.texte: List the supplies needed to clean the bathroom.
questions.reponse: The supplies needed are: a broom, a mop, cleaning solution, gloves, a scrub brush, and cloths.

questions.texte: What should you do before sweeping the floor?
questions.reponse: Before sweeping the floor, you should put on your gloves, remove all items from the sink, bathtub, and floor.

questions.texte: Explain the purpose of the scrub brush in this process.
questions.reponse: The scrub brush is used to remove stains from the sink and bathtub after applying the cleaning solution.

enonce.texte: You are preparing a weekly chore schedule for your family. Use your knowledge of household activities, phrasal verbs, and sequencing to complete the tasks below.
images: A weekly planner template with days of the week and space to write chores.

questions.texte: Write down at least five different household chores that need to be done every week. Use the correct vocabulary and phrasal verbs.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse :
- Sweep the kitchen floor.
- Wash the dishes after dinner.
- Dust the living room furniture.
- Clean the bathroom sinks and tub.
- Take out the garbage.

questions.texte: Create a weekly chore schedule. Assign specific days for each chore. Explain why you chose that day.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse :
Monday: Sweep the living room (because it gets dirty over the weekend).
Tuesday: Wash the dishes (daily after dinner).
Wednesday: Dust the furniture (remove dust buildup).
Thursday: Clean the bathroom (mid-week cleaning).
Friday: Take out the garbage (before the weekend).
Saturday: Mop all floors (deep cleaning).
Sunday: Organize and tidy up (prepare for the new week).

questions.texte: Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) describing how you and your family perform household chores together. Use at least three phrasal verbs and sequence words like 'first', 'next', 'then', 'finally'.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse :
First, we gather all the cleaning supplies such as brooms, mops, and cleaning solutions. Next, we divide the tasks: I look after the kitchen while my brother cleans the living room. Then, I sweep the floor and my sister wipes the windows. After that, we all work together to put everything back in its place. Finally, we look into any remaining mess and make sure the house is spotless. We enjoy working together because it makes the chores faster and more fun.

intitule: Giving and Following Instructions for Household Chores

enonce.texte: Complete the dialogue by filling in the gaps with the appropriate phrasal verbs or expressions related to household chores.
images: A cartoon image of a mother giving instructions to her child about cleaning the house.

questions.texte: Fill in the blanks with the correct phrasal verbs: look after, look for, look up, look into, look down
questions.reponse: The correct answers are provided below.

sousQuestions.texte: A: Can you please help me ______ my missing book? I need it for my homework.
B: Sure! But first, I need to ______ my little sister while Mom is cooking.
A: Okay. And after that, could you ______ the meaning of this word in the dictionary?
B: Yes. We also need to ______ the problem of the leaking faucet in the kitchen.
A: Absolutely. We should never ______ on people who don't help with chores. Everyone should participate.

questions.texte: Use sequence words to write instructions on how to clean the kitchen. Start with 'First' and end with 'Finally'.
questions.reponse:
Exemple de réponse :
First, clear the kitchen table and counters of any items. Next, wash the dishes in warm soapy water. Then, wipe down all surfaces with a clean cloth. After that, sweep the floor to remove dirt. Finally, mop the floor and put all items back in their proper places.
